QF.id: Clubbing, Set-Off and Carry Forward of Losses_17

question.text: Which of the following losses can be carried forward even if the return of income is NOT filed within the due date under Section 139(1)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The condition of filing the return on or before the due date under Section 139(1) applies to carry forward of: (i) business losses (speculative and non-speculative), and (ii) capital losses (STCL and LTCL). However, this condition does NOT apply to: (i) house property loss, and (ii) unabsorbed depreciation. These can be carried forward even with a belated return. **The Trap:** All four options are losses that can be carried forward, but only house property loss (and unabsorbed depreciation, not listed) are exempt from the timely filing condition. This is a commonly confused rule. **Cross-Exam Context:** The due date filing condition for carry forward is tested in both NISM X-A and X-B exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Create a simple 2x2 matrix for clients: (1) Losses requiring timely filing: business + capital, (2) Losses NOT requiring timely filing: house property + unabsorbed depreciation. This can save costly carry-forward lapses.
